title: SUMIF vs SUMPRODUCT: conditional sums two ways
meta_desc: SUMIF vs SUMPRODUCT for conditional sums: OR logic, computed conditions, cross-sheet quirks, speed, and compatibility across Excel, Google Sheets, and LibreOffice.
intro: SUMIF (and SUMIFS) cover the common cases fast; SUMPRODUCT is the escape hatch when conditions get interesting — OR logic, calculations inside the condition, or weighting. Knowing which is which saves both headaches and recalculation time.

table.aspect: Condition computed from the data (e.g. MONTH(dates)=7)

cols: Array evaluation — heavier on full columns
Universal (every version of all three apps we test)

when.use_when: Single literal condition — and reach for SUMIFS (not SUMPRODUCT) when you just need several ANDed literal conditions.
OR logic, conditions that require computing on the data first (dates by month, text lengths), or genuine sum-of-products like quantity x price.

compat_note: Both execute correctly in every version of all three apps we test — choose on capability, not compatibility. One portability note: SUMPRODUCT treats TRUE/FALSE arrays as 1/0 when multiplied or double-negated (--), and that behavior is identical across Excel, Sheets, and LibreOffice in our runs.

examples.label: SUMPRODUCT with OR (East or West, no double-count)
